name: MR Angiography Head
description: MR angiography of the intracranial arteries for non-invasive evaluation of vascular anatomy

findings.technique: Time-of-flight MR angiography was performed of the intracranial arteries without contrast administration.
findings.comparison: No prior studies available for comparison.

sections.name: Internal Carotid Arteries
sections.content: The bilateral internal carotid arteries demonstrate [normal caliber / no narrowing / no aneurysm]. The petrous and cavernous segments are [normal / show mild tortuosity].

sections.name: Anterior Cerebral Arteries
sections.content: The A1 segments of the bilateral anterior cerebral arteries are [present / hypoplastic]. The A2 segments and distal branches are [normal / symmetric].

sections.name: Middle Cerebral Arteries
sections.content: The M1 segments of the bilateral middle cerebral arteries demonstrate [normal caliber / no stenosis]. The M2 branches are [symmetric / normal].

sections.name: Posterior Cerebral Arteries
sections.content: The P1 segments of the bilateral posterior cerebral arteries are [present / hypoplastic / show fetal configuration]. The P2-P4 segments are [normal / patent].

sections.name: Vertebrobasilar System
sections.content: The vertebral arteries demonstrate [symmetric caliber / left dominance / right dominance / hypoplasia]. The basilar artery is [normal / shows fenestration]. No aneurysm.

sections.name: Circle of Willis
sections.content: The circle of Willis is [complete / incomplete]. The anterior communicating artery is [patent / absent]. The posterior communicating arteries are [patent / show fetal configuration].

sections.name: Aneurysm
sections.content: There is [no aneurysm / no saccular aneurysm / no fusiform dilation] identified at any location including the [MCA bifurcation / anterior communicating / posterior communicating / basilar apex].

sections.name: Stenosis
sections.content: There is [no flow-limiting stenosis / no significant atherosclerotic disease] in the intracranial arteries.

impression:
1. Normal MR angiography of the head.
2. The circle of Willis is anatomically complete.
3. No aneurysm or significant stenosis identified.
author: RadReport Templates
